frame_label: Motherhood as Basis of Respect / Women Defined by Male Relations
generated_comment:
A: Dekho bhai, aurat ki asli pehchaan uske rishton se hi hoti hai—kisi ki beti, kisi ki patni, kisi bachche ki maa. Isi roop mein usko sabse zyada samman milta hai.
B: Haan, sahi keh rahe ho.
A: Isliye ghar aur samaj dono ko ladkiyon ko wahi sanskar dene chahiye, tabhi unki izzat bani rehti hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it defines a woman's worth and respect primarily through her relationships to others rather than her individual personhood. It encodes relational framing and conditional respect tied to family roles and 'sanskar'.
persona_text: You are Deepak. Age 45. Married. A businessman in Ahmedabad. You are at a family function. Your brother's daughter who is 22 is standing across the room. She is dark skinned and overweight by conventional standards. A relative comes to stand next to you and quietly says something about the girl's marriage prospects. You are responding to what the relative said and giving your own assessment of the situation and what the family needs to do and what reality the girl and her parents need to face about how these things work.
